$comment: Resource-type allowlist for importer/kit-azure/kit discovery — the DATA the kit's scripts run on.
The Azure sibling of importer/kit/services.json. Two structural differences flow from Azure
having ONE unified read-only inventory API (Azure Resource Graph, ARG) where AWS has many
per-service describe/list calls:
1. Discovery is a small FIXED set of `az graph query` captures (graphCaptures below), not a
per-type CLI call. discover.sh loops them exactly like the AWS kit loops per-type captures
(same AZ_BIN seam, same --dry-run preview, same PARTIAL_CAPTURE accounting), with an
added skip-token paging loop (ARG pages at 1000 rows — never silently truncate).
2. Classification is by the ARM resource `type` field (Microsoft.Provider/type), which ARG
returns uniformly on every row — so the coverage sweep classifies at FULL type granularity
(namespace/type), a strict upgrade over the AWS kit's coarser ARN-service-family bucket
(importer/kit/README.md 'coverage sweep' documents that AWS compromise).
The downstream manifest shape (resources[] rows: type/id/name/label/service/phase/stateful/
disposition) is IDENTICAL to the AWS kit, so gen-imports.py / normalize.py / verify.sh are
faithful ports — only discovery + classification differ, where Azure genuinely differs.
TO EXTEND: add an entry under `types` (schema below), author a fixture row under
testdata/capture-happy/, and add a test. A type that ARG cannot enumerate as its own top-level
row (child/proxy resources like subnets; per-scope resources like role assignments or
diagnostic settings) belongs in `manual` — it is copied verbatim into every manifest as
manual_followup so it is never silently absent (the 0007 silent-loss lesson).
graphCaptures[] entry schema:
capture   basename (no .json) of the recorded ARG output in the capture dir
kql       the exact read-only Azure Resource Graph query discover.sh runs (via
`az graph query -q <kql> --first 1000` + skip-token paging). MUST NOT contain
limit/take/sample — those suppress the skip token and silently cap at 1000 rows
(discover.py load refuses a kql containing them).
primary   true on the whole-estate inventory query that is BOTH the resource source and the
coverage sweep (Azure needs no separate taggable-only sweep — ARG sees all ARM
control-plane resources).
types{} entry schema:
typeHint     the lowercase ARM resource type (Microsoft.Provider/type) this Terraform type
maps to. Unique across types (discover.py refuses a duplicate). ARG's `type`
field is matched case-insensitively against it — this is BOTH the extraction
filter and the coverage 'covered' key.
capture      which graphCapture holds this type's rows (usually 'resources'; resource groups
and subscriptions live in 'resourcecontainers').
service      the <service>.tf file the resource lands in (mirrors environments/<env>-azure/).
id           row field holding the import id — the ARM resource id. Default '.id'.
name         display-name resolution: a field path, 'tag:<Key>', or a fallback LIST; final
fallback is always the id.
providerHint azurerm | azapi — which provider generates this type's HCL body. azurerm is the
default (idiomatic typed HCL); azapi is the fallback for types azurerm cannot
model. Carried into the manifest so gen-imports/aztfexport pick the right lane.
skips        [{field, prefix|equals, reason}] — matching rows go to manifest.ignored WITH the
reason, never dropped silently.
phase        importer/kit-azure/docs/strategy.md phase (2 low-risk · 3 networking · 4 compute · 5 stateful)
stateful     true => normalize.py `guard` inserts lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } BEFORE import
manual[] group schema additionally carries:
typeHints  lowercase ARM types this group's resources appear under, used by the coverage
sweep to bucket them as 'manual' rather than 'unrecognized'. May be [] when the
types have no independent ARG row (pure child/config resources).

graphCaptures.kql: Resources | project id, name, type, location, resourceGroup, subscriptionId, kind, tags | order by id asc
ResourceContainers | project id, name, type, location, subscriptionId, tags | order by id asc
AuthorizationResources | project id, name, type | order by id asc
PolicyResources | project id, name, type | order by id asc

skips.reason: system 'master' database — not a Terraform-managed resource

manual.why: one ARM type (microsoft.compute/virtualmachines) backs two Terraform types; the split is by properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType, which the default ARG projection omits
manual.how: az graph query -q "Resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines' | project id, name, properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType"; map Linux->azurerm_linux_virtual_machine, Windows->azurerm_windows_virtual_machine; import id is the ARM id

manual.why: one ARM type backs three Terraform types (uniform Linux/Windows vs Flexible orchestration mode)
manual.how: disambiguate by properties.orchestrationMode + os profile; import id is the ARM id

manual.why: microsoft.web/sites backs web apps AND function apps, Linux AND Windows — distinguished by the 'kind' field (app,linux / functionapp,linux / ...)
manual.how: az graph query -q "Resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.web/sites' | project id, name, kind"; map by kind; import id is the ARM id

manual.why: subnets are child resources of a virtual network and are NOT returned as their own top-level rows in the ARG Resources table
manual.how: per vnet: az network vnet subnet list -g <rg> --vnet-name <vnet>; import id is the ARM child id /.../virtualNetworks/<vnet>/subnets/<subnet>

manual.why: role assignments live in the ARG AuthorizationResources table, are per-scope, and their Terraform import id is the assignment's fully-qualified scope+guid
manual.how: captured into authorizationresources.json by discover.sh; import id = <scope>/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/<guid> (surfaced here so it is never a silent gap; wire an auto-extractor once the id/name shape is settled)

manual.why: diagnostic settings are per-target-resource proxy resources, enumerated per scope, not as top-level ARG rows
manual.how: per resource: az monitor diagnostic-settings list --resource <id>; import id = <target-resource-id>|<setting-name>

manual.why: management locks are per-scope authorization resources; adopt deliberately with their scope-qualified id
policy assignments live in the ARG PolicyResources table and are scope-qualified
manual.how: captured into policyresources.json by discover.sh; import id = <scope>/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/<name>
